title: alter last partition failed when partition column is datetime
description:
## Bug Report

Please answer these questions before submitting your issue. Thanks!

### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required)

```sql
create table t (id int, create_time datetime)
		partition by range columns (create_time)
		interval (1 day)
		first partition less than ('2023-01-01')
		last partition less than ('2023-12-31');

alter table t last partition less than ('2024-01-01');
```

<!-- a step by step guide for reproducing the bug. -->

### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required)

execute success.

### 3. What did you see instead (Required)

got error:

```sql
> alter table t last partition less than ('2024-01-01');
(8200, 'Unsupported LAST PARTITION, does not seem like an INTERVAL partitioned table')
```

### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)

<!-- Paste the output of SELECT tidb_version() -->

```sql
tidb_version() | Release Version: v7.6.0-alpha-252-g3ed7732958
Edition: Community
Git Commit Hash: 3ed7732958bde87c200e0888a8e7268499b6a115
Git Branch: master
UTC Build Time: 2023-11-22 12:10:03
GoVersion: go1.21.0
Race Enabled: false
Check Table Before Drop: false
Store: unistore
```
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1 (id Int32, create_time DateTime) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY create_time PARTITION BY toYYYYMMDD(create_time);
CREATE TABLE t2 (id Int32, create_time DateTime NOT NULL, status String) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY create_time PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(create_time);
INSERT INTO t2 (id, create_time, status) VALUES (1, '2023-01-15 00:00:00', 'active'), (2, '2023-02-15 00:00:00', 'inactive');
SELECT * FROM t1 WHERE create_time IS NOT NULL;
key_bug_features: alter last partition with datetime column fails
